title: psyllophora pulicaris
intro: Psyllophora pulicaris, also known as Pulicaria, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pulicaria include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Pulicaria, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to treat various ailments, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
